full_text_rationale: Full text proposes Controlled Self-Evolution for algorithmic code optimization with Hierarchical Evolution Memory, including local intra-task lessons and global cross-task experience templates retrieved from a vector database, evaluated on EffiBench-X.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 008; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Tu Hu, Ronghao Chen, Shuo Zhang, Jianghao Yin, Mou Xiao Feng, Jingping Liu, Shaolei Zhang, Andy Wang, Wenqi Jiang, Yuqi Fang, Sen Hu, Huacan Wang, Yi Xu
titulo: Controlled Self-Evolution for Algorithmic Code Optimization
formato_armazenamento: Hierarchical Evolution Memory with local intra-task memory of improvement/failure lessons and global cross-task memory of compact experience items distilled from top improving/degrading evolution steps; entries are embedded with Qwen3-8B-Embedding and retrieved by cosine similarity.
framework_agente: Controlled Self-Evolution (CSE) for algorithmic code optimization
benchmarks_utilizados: EffiBench-X with 623 algorithmic problems in Python and C++
metricas_desempenho: Execution-time ratio, peak-memory ratio, memory-integral ratio versus human solutions, best-so-far MI, improvement count, iteration at best, last-10 improvement count, ablation metrics
baseline_comparado: Direct generation, Self-Reflection, SE-Agent, AlphaEvolve, ablations without diversified planning, genetic evolution, or hierarchical memory
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Local memory is compressed whenever it exceeds 1,000 tokens; for each processed task, top improving and degrading steps are distilled into global memory and added for subsequent task retrieval.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; fixed test-time budget of 30 candidates per task and no monetary/token cost per task reported.
resultado_principal: CSE consistently outperforms baselines on EffiBench-X across four LLM backbones. With GPT-5, CSE averages ET 67.70%, MP 65.62%, and MI 67.47%; with Claude-4.5-Sonnet, it averages ET 73.23%, MP 70.11%, and MI 74.41%.
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5, CSE improves average MI to 67.47% versus Direct 59.13%, Self-Reflection 60.24%, SE-Agent 64.41%, and AlphaEvolve 63.04%. Ablation on GPT-5 shows full CSE MI 68.10% versus 63.08% without memory, 63.82% without planning, and 64.72% without evolution.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; reports higher efficiency within the fixed 30-candidate budget but no monetary or token-cost comparison.
limitacoes: Does not yet distill CSE trajectories into model-weight RL training; multi-round evolution still incurs inference cost; results are algorithmic optimization specific.
ameacas_validade: EffiBench-X benchmark composition, candidate budget, fallback to Direct when budget exhausts, model/backbone selection, memory construction order, and embedding retrieval settings may affect validity.
